title: Born Again — A New Creation
intro: To be born again is not a religious phrase. It is a description of what actually happened to you the moment you placed your faith in Christ. Something ended. Something began. The old has passed away. Behold — the new has come.
reading:
Gavin's outline opens the identity section with what it means to be born again — because you cannot understand who you are in Christ until you understand what happened to you in Christ.

John 3:3 records Jesus telling Nicodemus — a religious leader, a man of position and knowledge — that unless one is born again he cannot even see the kingdom of God. This is not a call to try harder. It is a call to a new birth — something that can only happen from above, by the Spirit.

Titus 3:5 describes it as the washing of regeneration and renewing of the Holy Spirit. Regeneration is not renovation. It is not improvement. It is the creation of something entirely new where there was death before. The Holy Spirit does not upgrade the old nature — He gives a new one.

2 Corinthians 5:17 holds the most sweeping statement of what this means: if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away — it has gone. The new has come — it is here, now, standing. This is not positional language divorced from reality. It is the declaration of what has genuinely occurred in the deepest part of who you are.

Ephesians 4:22-24 calls us to live from that new self — created after the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness. Colossians 3:10 adds that the new self is being renewed in knowledge after the image of its creator.

The new birth is not the end. It is the beginning of a lifelong transformation into the image of Christ.
reflection: Do you tend to live from the identity of the old self or the new? What lies or old patterns still feel more true to you than the reality of what Christ has made you?
abide: Lord, I am a new creation. Help me to live from that truth today — not from the old story I keep returning to, but from the new life You spoke into existence when You made me Your own.
